---
title: "`r params$report_title`"
author: "`r params$report_author`"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Engine flag: TRUE only when this template is stitched into the
  # combined report (set by the parent). Leave false for normal renders.
  combined_mode: false
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: !r here::here("1_data", "master_micro_survey.csv")
  
  # Variables
  outcome_var: "baseline_score"
  group_var: "treatment_group"
  
  # Policy-Facing Labels
  group1_label: "Treatment Group"    
  group2_label: "Control Group"   
  metric_name: "Baseline Test Score"

  # --- Report Text (all editable; leave "" to use the smart default) ---
  report_title: "Distribution Shifts: Density Plot"
  report_author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
  chart_title: ""
  chart_subtitle: ""
  x_label: ""
  source_note: "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = !isTRUE(params$combined_mode),
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev.args = list(bg = "#FAF9F6"),
  fig.path = paste0(here::here("4_output", "figures"), "/"),
  fig.width = 9,
  fig.height = 6, 
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r cerp-style, echo=FALSE, results='asis', eval=!isTRUE(params$combined_mode)}
cat("<style>", readr::read_file(here::here("2_R", "cerp_style.css")), "</style>")
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)

source(here::here("2_R", "2.5_helpers.R"), local = TRUE)

# Load data (headers/values whitespace-scrubbed) and validate YAML params
raw_data <- cerp_load(params$data_path)
cerp_validate(raw_data, params[grepl("_var$", names(params))])

# ==============================================================================
# DATA SCRUBBING & PROCESSING BLOCK
# ==============================================================================
plot_data <- raw_data %>%
  # 1. Safely drop NAs only for the required variables
  drop_na(!!sym(params$outcome_var), !!sym(params$group_var)) %>%
  
  # 2. Scrub and standardize text inputs
  mutate(
    Clean_Group = str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(!!sym(params$group_var)))),
    
    # Intelligently map messy data to the clean Policy Labels defined in YAML
    Group = case_when(
      str_detect(Clean_Group, "(?i)Treat|Intervention|1|Yes|Public") ~ str_wrap(params$group1_label, width = 25),
      TRUE ~ str_wrap(params$group2_label, width = 25)
    )
  ) %>%
  
  # 3. Lock factor levels so Group 1 always plots vertically on top of Group 2
  mutate(
    Group = factor(Group, levels = c(
      str_wrap(params$group1_label, width = 25), 
      str_wrap(params$group2_label, width = 25)
    ))
  )

# 4. Calculate medians dynamically to draw the dashed reference lines
median_data <- plot_data %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  summarize(
    median_val = median(!!sym(params$outcome_var), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```


```{r plot-distribution}
source(here::here("2_R", "2.0_setup_theme.R"), local = TRUE)

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = !!sym(params$outcome_var))) +
  
  # Draw the density curves, filled with the CERP primary color
  geom_density(fill = cerp_cols["primary"], color = NA, alpha = 0.8) +
  
  # Add the heavy dashed line for the median
  geom_vline(data = median_data, aes(xintercept = median_val), 
             color = cerp_cols["accent"], linewidth = 1.2, linetype = "dashed") +
  
  # Stack the plots vertically based on our scrubbed grouping variable
  facet_wrap(~Group, ncol = 1) +
  
  theme_cerp() +
  
  # Strip the Y-axis text (non-technical readers do not need to read raw density decimals)
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold") # Format the facet labels cleanly
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = if (nzchar(params$chart_title)) params$chart_title else (paste("Distribution Spread:", params$metric_name)),
    subtitle = if (nzchar(params$chart_subtitle)) params$chart_subtitle else ("Comparing outcomes across groups. The dashed yellow line represents the median."),
    x = if (nzchar(params$x_label)) params$x_label else (params$metric_name),
    caption = params$source_note
  )
```
